author: @dinnade9338
text:
Hi Robert 
Congrats for your 2000 !!! Love it. I am a big fan of yours. So far I haven't bothered asking questions ... but this time I have few questions , Hope you would be able to help me.
I have seen your 1929 -serpentine coil... and  other stuff ..but ;  

1. Why do we have to keep a hole in AXIAL FLUX COIL.  ( Coreless design ) 
2. Let say  : I have  25mm Diameter / Height 15mm  - Cylinder Magnet  --->   What is the best way to wind the stator coil  for that shape ?   ( in order to get max magnetic flux )

I really appreciate if you could help on this ..  
Have a great day ... looking forward to see your 2500th video.. 

Be Happy .. Dinna

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Hiya Dinna - you don't have to keep a hole - it s just a practical thing - hard to wind it unless there is something to wind it on - you should review a couple of the vids on understanding generators that i made - it will help

replies.author: @dinnade9338
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  Thank you for your quick response. Enjoy your day .. be happy

text: Congratulations sir. I appreciate all your efforts to make science into reality. I just want some support from you in getting details about some of your project. Please let us know how can we connect with you. Maybe over an email or WhatsApp, whatever suits you.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am sorry mate but i get something in the order of 3-400 emails, comments and posts every day - i already spend 2 hours a day replying - I am not trying to be a jerk but it really isn't possible for me to give individual help and most folks problems are the same - if you post a comment i will try and answer it that way everyone else who has the same problem gets to share in the answer. Also, if you post a comment you will find others will try and help too

author: @brewswillas6635
text: Thank you, Robert, for so many wonderful videos. Honestly, in this world of constant commercialism, your videos are the breath of hope. author: @Blue_Baloo
text: Robert, I don't usually comment but felt I needed to say congratulations on 2000 and thank you for providing your brilliant ideas and your enthusiasm/cheerful disposition to the world. I am not surprised others are taking your designs and running with them, and kudos to you for being so open about sharing, posting your designs, and allowing people to further innovate. I am working on a modified version of  the wind collector + mag lev turbine at the bottom to stick in my window to take advantage of a cross-breeze through the house, but I didn't know anything about generators or turbines to start with. Your videos provided a solid foundation to learn the basic concepts by actually building each piece to understand how it works myself. I really resonate with what you were saying about not being afraid to try something new and learn the necessary skills to make it happen. Thanks again for all you have taught us and looking forward to the next one!
